summary_text: **Top Findings (Critical First)** - **High: Split-brain risk if Supabase becomes a second authority.** Your own contract already says Notion is the operational/editable source of truth, and Supabase is supplemental. If quoted-author state is writable in both places, drift is guaranteed. Refs: [x-auto-contract.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/docs/agents/x-auto-contract.md#L41), [x-auto-contract.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/docs/agents/x-auto-contract.md#L43), [x-auto-note-linking-rule.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/docs/x-auto-note-linking-rule.md#L10) - **High: Privacy/compliance scope can explode fast if you track people, not references.** “Quoted-author tracking” easily drifts into personal profiling (handles, post history, inferred stance, engagement metrics). That creates retention/deletion/legal-basis obligations you likely do not need for v1. - **High: Service-role blast radius is large.** Current Supabase bridge contract uses service-role key at adapter boundary. If v1 adds broad author tables + write paths, one key compromise can mutate all tracking data. Ref: [supabase-rest-bridge.json](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/config/integrations/contracts/supabase-rest-bridge.json#L10) - **Medium: Overengineering risk from premature normalization.** A full `authors`, `aliases`, `posts`, `topics`, `relations` graph in v1 is likely wasteful. You don’t have stable identity resolution rules yet (URL canonicalization, cross-platform aliases, renamed handles). - **Medium: Pipeline fragility if posting depends on tracking writes.** If quote tracking is on the critical path, Supabase latency/outage can block draft generation/posting. In v1 this should be non-blocking telemetry, not gating logic. - **Medium: False diversity signal.** You can “add sources/topics” and still produce same conclusion templates unless generation rules change. This is a model-policy issue, not just a datastore issue. --- **Leaner v1 Recommendation** 1. Keep **Notion as the only writable source** for queue row state (`Body`, `Body JA`, `Source URLs`, `Status`, scheduling). 2. Add Supabase as a **read-model sidecar only** for analytics: one append-only table like `quote_references` with minimal fields: `notion_page_id`, `source_url`, `source_domain`, `quote_mode`, `created_at`, `hash`. 3. Use **one-way ingestion** Notion -> Supabase (idempotent upsert by `notion_page_id + source_url hash`), never reverse sync for content fields. 4. Store **no author profile enrichment** in v1 (no bio scraping, no follower stats, no embeddings). 5. Make tracking **fail-open**: if Supabase write fails, posting pipeline continues and logs retry. 6. Add basic compliance controls now: retention TTL, delete-by-`notion_page_id`, and audit log for writes. --- **What To Avoid In v1** - Dual-write (Notion + Supabase both editable for quoted-author state) - Event bus/CDC + realtime consumers before idempotent batch ingest is stable - Full identity graph (author master, alias resolution, cross-network joins) - Feeding raw quoted text directly into generation without sanitization/prompt-injection controls - Any ranking/approval decisions based on immature quote-author scores
